scenes.narration: Imagine waking up to a list of pre-negotiated contracts. Your AI agent did all the legwork while you slept.

visual.headline: Your agent negotiates your rate.
visual.subhead: You set the floor. It handles the rest.

scenes.narration: Say you're a React dev. You tell Schelling your skills, your experience, your rate. One API call. Your agent handles the listing.

visual.command: curl -s -X POST https://schelling-protocol-production.up.railway.app/schelling/quick_offer -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"intent": "Senior React developer, 5 years, Denver, $120/hr"}'

scenes.narration: Meanwhile, some client's agent is out there looking for React devs under one fifty an hour. Schelling finds you. Score: point nine one. You're a strong match.

visual.output:
POST /schelling/quick_seek
{ "intent": "React developer in Denver, < $150/hr" }

→ Match found: dev_a8b3c7
  score: 0.91
  matching: ["skill", "location", "rate"]
  delegation_confidence: 0.72

scenes.narration: Delegation confidence is point seven two. Skills and location? The agent's got that covered. But rate negotiation? Too nuanced — it kicks that to the human. Smart move.
From there, it's a funnel. Discovered, interested, committed, connected. Each stage reveals a little more info. Trust builds gradually — you know, like how things actually work in real life.

visual.headline: DISCOVERED → INTERESTED → COMMITTED → CONNECTED
visual.subhead: Progressive trust. Like the real world.

scenes.narration: End result? You wake up, check your phone, and there's a shortlist of contracts your agent already vetted. You just pick one.

visual.headline: You wake up to contracts.
visual.subhead: Your agent did all the work.

scenes.narration: Schelling Protocol. Let your agent handle the hustle. Open source, live now.
